title: Leisure and theme park attendants
description: All artists and related workers not listed separately. Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies). All entertainers and performers, sports and related workers not listed separately. (Based on 26 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Perform general cleaning of buildings or properties, maintain order and ensure adherence to safety rules, and assist patrons by giving directions to points in or outside of the facility or providing information about local attractions.

Clean and press costumes before and after performances and perform any minor repairs. Assign lockers to employees and maintain locker rooms, dressing rooms, wig rooms, or costume storage or laundry areas.

Design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for construction, major repairs, or alterations. Purchase, rent, or requisition costumes or other wardrobe necessities. Check the appearance of costumes on stage or under lights to determine whether desired effects are being achieved. Inventory stock to determine types or conditions of available costuming. Collaborate with production designers, costume designers, or other production staff to discuss and execute costume design details. Monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, providing keys or access to assigned directors, costume designers, or wardrobe mistresses/masters.

Move levers, depress foot pedals, or turn dials to operate cranes, cherry pickers, electromagnets, or other moving equipment for lifting, moving, or placing loads. Determine load weights and check them against lifting capacities to prevent overload. Move levers, pedals, and throttles to stop, start, and regulate speeds of hoist or winch drums. Start engines of hoists or winches and use levers and pedals to wind or unwind cable on drums. Observe equipment gauges and indicators and hand signals of other workers to verify load positions or depths. Operate compressed air, diesel, electric, gasoline, or steam-driven hoists or winches to control movement of cableways, cages, derricks, draglines, loaders, railcars, or skips. Move or reposition hoists, winches, loads and materials, manually or using equipment and machines.

Perform maintenance duties, such as sweeping, painting, and yard work to keep facilities clean and in order. Record details of attendance, sales, receipts, reservations, or repair activities. Provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations. Maintain clean lobbies or entrance areas for travelers or guests. Monitor activities to ensure adherence to rules and safety procedures, or arrange for the removal of unruly patrons.

Provide dressing assistance to cast members or assign cast dressers to assist specific cast members with costume changes. Sell tickets and collect fees from customers. Transfer luggage, trunks, and packages to and from rooms, loading areas, vehicles, or transportation terminals, by hand or using baggage carts. Transport guests about premises and local areas, or arrange for transportation. Supply guests or travelers with directions, travel information, and other information, such as available services and points of interest. Greet incoming guests and escort them to their rooms. Assist travelers and guests with disabilities. Direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions. Keep informed of shut-down and emergency evacuation procedures.

Clean and lubricate equipment, and make minor repairs and adjustments. Inspect equipment to detect wear and damage and perform minor repairs, adjustments, or maintenance tasks, such as oiling parts. Fasten safety devices for patrons, or provide them with directions for fastening devices. Provide assistance to patrons entering or exiting amusement rides, boats, or ski lifts, or mounting or dismounting animals. Announce or describe amusement park attractions to patrons to entice customers to games and other entertainment. Operate, drive, or explain the use of mechanical riding devices or other automatic equipment in amusement parks, carnivals, or recreation areas. Schedule the use of recreation facilities, such as golf courses, tennis courts, bowling alleys, or softball diamonds.

Collect and record data associated with operating geothermal power plants or well fields. Determine whether emergency or auxiliary systems will be needed to keep properties heated or cooled in extreme weather conditions. Monitor and adjust operations of geothermal power plant equipment or systems. Identify and correct malfunctions of geothermal plant equipment, electrical systems, instrumentation, or controls. Prepare and maintain logs, reports, or other documentation of work performed.

Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers. Examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements. Load vehicles with listed goods, ensuring goods are loaded correctly and taking precautions with hazardous goods. Unload and sort items collected along delivery routes. Plan and follow the most efficient routes for delivering goods. Deliver messages and items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, between establishment departments and to other establishments and private homes. Perform routine maintenance on delivery vehicles, such as monitoring fluid levels and replenishing fuel.

Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials. Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers. Record information, such as items received and delivered and recipients' responses to messages. Check with home offices after completed deliveries to confirm deliveries and collections and to receive instructions for other deliveries.

Inspect canal and bridge equipment, and areas, such as roadbeds, for damage or defects, reporting problems to supervisors as necessary. Conduct sound checks to ensure equipment is working and appropriate for the venue. Check that bridges are clear of vehicles and pedestrians prior to opening. Collect payments from customers. Verify, collect, or punch tickets before admitting patrons to venues, such as amusement parks and rides.

Load, unload, or position lighting equipment. Install electrical cables or wire fixtures. Match light fixture settings, such as brightness and color, to lighting design plans. Notify supervisors when major lighting equipment repairs are needed. Consult with lighting director or production staff to determine lighting requirements. Test lighting equipment function and desired lighting effects. Disassemble and store equipment after performances.

Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to telephone or radio reports of emergencies, plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet operational goals, and resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution.

Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications; maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities; and enforce safety rules and regulations.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents, and analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports.
Compute or estimate cash, payroll, transportation, personnel, or storage requirements, and requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems, and resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Recommend or implement personnel actions, such as employee selection, evaluation, rewards, or disciplinary actions, and recruit and hire staff members.
Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, work methods, or customer services, and participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.

Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies, and observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Interpret transportation or tariff regulations, shipping orders, safety regulations, or company policies and procedures for workers.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers, such as flight or car attendants, and ensure that personnel follow established standards.

Inspect crane site conditions to determine ground stability.
